questions.specification:
Figure 1 shows an experiment being conducted on a material specimen.

![Figure 1](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/notes/93dc53fa-8c43-4bad-b732-115b527e0cc2.jpeg)

What type of material property does this experiment explore?

options.markscheme: Electrical properties relate to a material's ability to conduct or resist electricity. This setup involves physical pulling, not electrical measurement.
Mechanical properties describe how a material responds to applied forces. The tensile test shown measures properties such as strength, elasticity, and ductility.
Aesthetic properties relate to the appearance and sensory qualities of a material (e.g., color, texture), which are not being quantitatively measured in a tensile test.
Magnetic properties involve a material's response to a magnetic field, which is not being tested in a mechanical pulling experiment.

questions.specification: Which of the following best describes a physical property?

options.content: A measurable characteristic that does not change the substance
A property that relates to resistance to corrosion
A feature dependent on market trends

questions.specification: Which of the following best describes a smart material?

options.content: A material that biodegrades after disposal
A material formed by combining natural and synthetic sources
A material that conducts electricity at room temperature
A material that changes properties in response to external stimuli

questions.specification: Which of the following **metals** would most likely be selected based on aesthetic appeal (a polished finish) and low density?

options.markscheme: Not correct: carbon steel has a relatively high density compared with aluminium.
Correct: aluminium has a low density for a metal and can be given a high-quality polished finish for aesthetic appeal.
Not correct: oak is not a metal.
Not correct: thermoplastic elastomer is not a metal and is not typically selected for a polished metallic aesthetic finish.

questions.specification:
Which type of property is demonstrated when a metal reacts with an acid?

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXfMH7Vb47RSgMxPM9EPNLlrDEF-A0devcHE4QUaxn_vM22HuiY1h4khv3Aa90_V0BNyOzC3vv7V3Y20RwGSeO21TstI9JfYvRRoPIeIRXHYAbiWAebgcnrVnnqcRGezbfiAAAmj?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)

Which source of material is represented here?

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXenVQ9J1IFxzpqjju4FtP8mi3q0W17iBb_AFrlZO8BJVwd6cRutqgX4x5i_iEs8A3Kx4d7pS8LwnzJwuEP7TP7vsbRqt9RZhc5ihnTd2gGQ-LabXYXcYJv19LBEpGkb44RZb7qKHg?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXcWCcNstWvbaKl44nEacsrtKGCY6fcKNrsIg2DuU_Dt0E_rNtNBS5HT_y8-XR6ISMiZTBEYWgzgbDU56OHocI2DU7nTzkIdkEMiedsnJnRYGsU6z-loVNypciMw7L4PLg-AGR5TgA?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)
Why are biodegradable materials increasingly used in product design?

options.content: They are commonly used in metal alloys
They naturally decompose, reducing environmental impact

questions.specification: A design company is developing a premium kitchen knife set that includes a chef’s knife, a wooden storage block, and a protective blade cover. The designers carefully select materials to balance sharpness, durability, aesthetics, and safety.

parts.content:
The knife blade is made from high-carbon stainless steel.

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/c9ee4988-44cd-43ae-89b9-ca3b87beb407)

Identify one mechanical property of the material used in **Figure 1** that makes it suitable for a knife blade.
parts.markscheme:
- High toughness to resist chipping/fracture during use <marks>1 mark</marks>

_**OR**_

- High stiffness/strength so the blade resists bending in use <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
The knife block is made from solid beech wood.

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/ebbd796c-1f99-437c-98ad-9f0f4167fbd6)

i.) State one physical property of beech wood that is useful for the knife block.
parts.markscheme:
- Low thermal conductivity/acts as an insulator (comfortable/safer to handle and store against) <marks>1 mark</marks>

_**OR**_

- Suitable density (gives a stable, weighty block) <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: ii.) Outline two advantages of using beech wood instead of plastic for the storage block shown in **Figure 2**.
parts.markscheme:
- Renewable and biodegradable material <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Has natural antibacterial properties <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
The blade cover is made from flexible thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU).

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/b63f2545-2764-4bb3-8bce-09278f311f9a)

Explain why TPU is an appropriate material for the blade cover shown in **Figure 3**.
parts.markscheme:
- It is flexible and provides a snug fit over the blade <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Resistant to abrasion and puncture, increasing safety <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Transparent TPU allows users to view the blade edge <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Compare the origin and classification of the materials used in **Figures 1–3**.
parts.markscheme:
- Stainless steel is a ferrous alloy derived from iron ore <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Beech wood is a natural material from trees (organic source) <marks>1 mark</marks>
- TPU is a synthetic thermoplastic polymer <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Each represents a different material classification (metal, wood, polymer) <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Discuss how the material selection in **Figures 1–3** balances functionality and environmental impact.
parts.markscheme:
- Stainless steel provides cutting efficiency and corrosion resistance for long-term use (Figure 1) <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Beech wood is sustainable, biodegradable, and aesthetically pleasing (Figure 2) <marks>1 mark</marks>
- TPU is durable and enhances safety but is non-biodegradable (Figure 3) <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Overall material choices support reusability and product longevity <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Aesthetic and tactile qualities improve user satisfaction and reduce replacement rates <marks>1 mark</marks>
- The environmental impact is mitigated through thoughtful material pairing <marks>1 mark</marks>

questions.specification: Which of the following best explains why material selection is challenging for designers?

options.content: It is driven mostly by fashion cycles
It depends solely on supplier availability
It involves balancing properties with aesthetics and context

questions.specification: A design company is creating a range of portable picnic accessories for outdoor use. The team is prototyping three products: a folding picnic table, reusable cutlery, and an insulated food container. They must carefully select materials based on origin, mechanical and physical properties, and sustainability.

parts.content:
Designers are comparing material options for a folding picnic table, which must be lightweight yet strong enough to support food and drinks outdoors.

![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/912a8430-a40e-48d2-bd10-a0dc78d4642d)

Identify the most appropriate material origin for each of the following picnic table materials in **Figure 1**: Powder-coated aluminium, Recycled HDPE and Plywood.
parts.markscheme:
- Powder-coated aluminium – Synthetic <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Recycled HDPE plastic – Synthetic <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Plywood – Composite <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Award 1 mark for each correct identification based on material origin <marks>0 mark</marks>

parts.content: State one advantage of using plywood instead of natural timber for the tabletop.
parts.markscheme:
- Plywood has increased dimensional stability and is less prone to warping. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Award 1 mark for a valid property-based advantage of plywood. <marks>0 mark</marks>

parts.content:
The team has selected stainless steel for reusable picnic cutlery that can withstand repeated washing and resist wear.

![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/155cf331-ada6-491a-850d-e75dbb326d13)

Compare the mechanical properties of stainless steel and polycarbonate for picnic cutlery.
parts.markscheme:
- Stainless steel is stronger and more durable under repeated mechanical stress. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- It is more resistant to bending and scratching. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Polycarbonate is lighter and has good impact resistance. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- However, it may degrade faster with frequent dishwashing. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Award 1 mark for each relevant comparison of strength, durability, and wear resistance. <marks>0 mark</marks>

parts.content: Explain why polycarbonate is suitable for reusable cutlery despite being a plastic.
parts.markscheme:
- Polycarbonate has high impact resistance and does not shatter easily. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- It can withstand boiling water and dishwasher cycles. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- It is lightweight and easy to mold into utensil shapes. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Award 1 mark for each valid explanation supported by a relevant property. <marks>0 mark</marks>

parts.content:
The design team is choosing insulation materials for a food container to maintain food temperature outdoors.

![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/5accdf90-fa3d-4c90-8e84-a2da46076776)

Suggest two reasons why polyurethane foam is used as the core insulating material for the food container.
parts.markscheme:
- It has very low thermal conductivity, which reduces heat transfer. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- It is lightweight, adding minimal weight to the product. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Award 1 mark for each appropriate reason based on thermal or physical properties. <marks>0 mark</marks>

parts.content: Discuss the suitability of using bioplastics for the outer shell of the container, based on environmental and material performance factors.
parts.markscheme:
- Bioplastics reduce reliance on fossil fuels. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- They are biodegradable under industrial conditions, reducing landfill waste. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Some types have acceptable impact resistance and thermal properties. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- However, bioplastics may not be as durable under UV exposure. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- They may deform or crack at high temperatures. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Not all bioplastics decompose easily in natural conditions. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Award 1 mark for each well-reasoned point, balanced across advantages and disadvantages. <marks>0 mark</marks>

questions.specification: Which pair represents chemical properties?

options.markscheme: These are physical properties as they can be observed or measured without changing the chemical identity of the substance.
These are physical properties. Density is a ratio of mass to volume, and hardness relates to resistance to deformation; neither requires a chemical change to measure.
These are physical properties that can be observed directly through the senses without altering the substance's chemical structure.

Chemical properties describe the ability of a substance to undergo a specific chemical change. Flammability (reaction with oxygen) and corrosion resistance (resistance to oxidation/reaction) are both chemical properties.

questions.specification:
This image best illustrates which physical property?

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/notes/a03a9bb8-8f94-4564-9777-75acdf5dcdd6.jpeg)

options.markscheme: Hardness is the resistance a material offers to penetration or scratching, which is not demonstrated in the image.
Colourfastness refers to the resistance of a material to fading or running, usually relevant to textiles and dyes.
Transparency is the physical property of allowing light to pass through the material so that objects behind it can be distinctly seen, as shown by the clear visibility of the grid through the block.
Density is the mass per unit volume of a material; it cannot be determined simply by looking at a single object without measurement data.

questions.specification: A start-up company is developing a school lunchbox set aimed at being durable, safe for food contact, and environmentally friendly. Each component is made from a carefully selected material based on its classification and properties.

parts.content:
The lunchbox container is made from injection-moulded polypropylene (PP).

![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/333a08fa-3566-469f-9980-17d821704816)

Identify one chemical property of the material used for the lunchbox in **Figure 1**.
parts.markscheme:
Food-safe (non-reactive with food) <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for a valid chemical property.*

parts.content:
The cutlery is made from a reusable bamboo composite.

![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/517053d2-41ed-4633-ae19-6d9a9a18d3eb)

Define the term composite material using the cutlery in **Figure 2** as context.
parts.markscheme:
A composite is made by combining two or more materials to enhance overall properties. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for a clear and relevant definition.*

parts.content: State two advantages of using a bamboo composite for the cutlery shown in **Figure 2.**
parts.markscheme:
Bamboo is biodegradable and renewable <marks>1 mark</marks>

The composite increases strength and durability compared to pure bamboo <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] per valid advantage.*

parts.content:
The lunchbox also includes a flexible lid seal made from silicone rubber.

![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/27e7e287-376f-40e9-9455-9e3df9499c2f)

Explain how the mechanical properties of the silicone in **Figure 3** make it suitable for this component.
parts.markscheme:
It is flexible and stretchable for a tight seal <marks>1 mark</marks>

It returns to shape after deformation (elasticity) <marks>1 mark</marks>

It resists tearing during repeated use (toughness) <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award up to [3] for relevant mechanical properties and link to function.*

parts.content: Compare the material origins of the three components shown in **Figures 1–3**.
parts.markscheme:
Polypropylene is a synthetic, human-made polymer <marks>1 mark</marks>

Bamboo composite includes natural fibres with a synthetic resin <marks>1 mark</marks>

Silicone rubber is a synthetic elastomer <marks>1 mark</marks>

Identifies differences in classification and sustainability impact <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award up to [4] for accurate classification comparisons supported by examples.*

parts.content: Discuss how the classification and properties of the materials used in **Figures 1–3** contribute to both usability and sustainability of the lunchbox set.
parts.markscheme:
PP is lightweight, food-safe, and recyclable, ideal for main container (Figure 1) <marks>1 mark</marks>

Bamboo composite enhances cutlery strength while being biodegradable (Figure 2) <marks>1 mark</marks>

Silicone lid ensures leak-proof seal and durability through flexibility (Figure 3) <marks>1 mark</marks>

Classification by mechanical, chemical, and physical properties supports safe reuse <marks>1 mark</marks>

Choices reflect user-friendly design and alignment with eco-conscious values <marks>1 mark</marks>

Materials also enable cost-effective mass production with low environmental impact <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award up to [6] for well-supported discussion with consistent reference to figures.*

questions.specification:
The image shows an experiment determining the thermal expansion of a certain metal. Which property is being assessed when checking how much a material expands when heated?

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXc_VWAz-fFmlxUWMErSDmU8gJ5ibfaatB2uXkvKrEzvWG4pubegaNpl-0QXSfuDWAVyXYeVUmSdlvGmcjodhQ8u9dmPsumALR4HNpYYFp5t7Bw_AQ8F2deFyCzD41M7hXf1i4hWVQ?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)
A product must endure high tensile and compressive forces. What material property should be prioritized?

parts.content: Discuss the implications of using biodegradable and smart materials in the development of sustainable consumer products.
parts.markscheme:
- Biodegradable materials help reduce environmental waste post-use by breaking down into natural elements rather than remaining in landfills. <marks>1</marks>

- Smart materials can optimize product lifespans (e.g., self-healing coatings), reducing the frequency of replacement and resource consumption. <marks>1</marks>

- The use of these materials encourages circular economy practices by focusing on renewable resources or extended product life cycles. <marks>1</marks>

- Promotes consumer awareness and eco-branding, allowing companies to differentiate products based on their sustainability profile and environmental impact. <marks>1</marks>

- Technical and economic challenges must be addressed, such as the lower durability of some biodegradable plastics or the high initial R&D costs of smart materials. <marks>1</marks>

- Life cycle assessment (LCA) is complex; it must account for energy in production and the specific conditions required for effective end-of-life disposal. <marks>1</marks>

- Positive impact on regulatory compliance, helping manufacturers meet international environmental standards and laws (e.g., EU packaging directives). <marks>1</marks>

- Future innovation potential, such as piezoelectric materials generating power (e.g., in footwear) or shape memory alloys improving functional efficiency. <marks>1</marks>

- A concluding statement weighing the overall environmental benefits against practical limitations and economic costs. <marks>1</marks>

*Award [1] per relevant implication with explanation. Balanced arguments and real-world examples are required for full marks, up to [9 max].*

questions.specification: IB Design Technology: Topic 2 - Resource management and sustainable production

parts.content: Define the term “biodegradable material” in the context of product design.
parts.markscheme:
- **State** that a biodegradable material is one that breaks down naturally through biological processes or the action of microorganisms. <marks>1</marks>
- **Outline** that these materials decompose after disposal or at the end of their useful life. <marks>1</marks>

<note>
Award [1] for a basic definition and [1] for a contextual elaboration or example, up to [2 marks].
</note>

2 marks total

questions.specification: Why are composites often selected in aerospace and automotive industries?

options.markscheme: Chemical inertness is not the primary driver for selection in these industries compared to the weight-to-strength ratio.

options.content: They are lighter but mechanically stronger than traditional materials.
options.markscheme: Composites provide a high strength-to-weight ratio (specific strength), which is essential for fuel efficiency and performance in aerospace and automotive applications.
Composites typically require post-processing steps such as curing, trimming, and surface finishing.
Structural composites like carbon fiber reinforced polymers are generally opaque and not biodegradable.

questions.specification: What distinguishes composites from other material types?

options.content: They are made from two or more materials combined for enhanced properties
They are fully recyclable and natural
They are always stronger than smart materials

questions.specification: A material that increases stiffness when exposed to an electric field is most likely a:
How are materials classified according to their origin?

options.markscheme: Melting point is a physical property, not a classification of origin.
These are mechanical and physical properties of materials.
Materials are classified by their resource origin into renewable (replenishable naturally), non-renewable (finite resources), and recycled (reprocessed from waste).
This classification refers to the composition and structure of the material rather than the resource origin.

parts.content: Discuss the role of physical properties when selecting materials for thermal insulation products.
parts.markscheme:
- **State** that physical properties such as thermal conductivity and density are critical for effective material selection <marks>A1</marks>

- **Explain** that materials with low thermal conductivity, such as foam or fiberglass, are preferred for insulation to minimize heat transfer <marks>R1</marks>

- **Outline** how density affects the mass and handling of insulation products during the installation process <marks>R1</marks>

- **Analyze** how designers consider these factors to optimize energy efficiency and overall product usability <marks>R1</marks>

- **Identify** that improper material selection can result in significant thermal losses and decreased efficiency <marks>A1</marks>

- **Justify** the selection of specific materials through the use of contextual examples <marks>R1</marks>

6 marks total

<note>Award [1] for each valid point with explanation, up to [6 max].</note>

questions.specification: Which of the following is an example of a physical property?

options.markscheme: Reaction with acids describes a chemical change where a substance is transformed into different substances, making it a chemical property.
Reactivity with water indicates a substance's potential to undergo a chemical reaction, which is a chemical property.
Density is a physical property because it can be observed and measured (mass per unit volume) without changing the chemical composition of the substance.
Flammability describes the ability of a substance to burn in the presence of oxygen, resulting in a chemical change; therefore, it is a chemical property.

questions.specification: What defines the mechanical property of toughness?

options.content: Ability to absorb energy without fracturing

questions.specification: A designer is comparing three materials for a load-bearing structure. One is lightweight but chemically unstable, another is very strong but highly reactive, and the third is moderately strong, stable, and cost-effective. Which set of material properties is most relevant to this decision?
A designer selects a material based on its ability to bend without breaking. This decision is based on:

In IB Design Technology terms, what property is being observed when this material bends without breaking?

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/notes/87c81ed4-91ef-465e-a48f-b42e7de6b5fe.jpeg)

parts.content: Explain how smart materials respond to external stimuli, using one example.
parts.markscheme:
- Smart materials change one or more of their properties in response to external stimuli <marks>A1</marks>
- Common stimuli include environmental changes such as temperature, light, or pressure <marks>A1</marks>
- Identification of a valid example of a smart material (e.g. Shape Memory Alloy / SMA) <marks>A1</marks>
- Explanation of the specific response to the stimulus (e.g. the material returns to its original shape when heated) <marks>A1</marks>

*Award **[1]** for a general definition of smart materials, **[1]** for identifying potential stimuli, **[1]** for naming a specific smart material, and **[1]** for explaining its response to a stimulus.*

questions.specification: A design team is developing a line of school supplies that are sustainable, safe, and durable for everyday student use. The team is evaluating materials for three products: a plastic ruler, a metal compass, and a mechanical pencil.

parts.content:
The team selects polycarbonate (PC) for the transparent ruler to ensure visibility and impact resistance.

![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/fc1f4fd8-88d6-468e-aa30-74ce0468d0df)

Identify one mechanical property of polycarbonate that makes it suitable for the ruler.
parts.markscheme:
High impact resistance prevents shattering when dropped <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for a relevant mechanical property.*

parts.content: Explain why transparency is a beneficial physical property for the ruler.
parts.markscheme:
Allows students to see markings and underlying work clearly <marks>1 mark</marks>

Aids precision in drawing and measuring <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for each correct explanation.*

parts.content:
The compass is constructed with an aluminium body for its light weight and corrosion resistance.

![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/176a70f6-82a3-48fb-8d66-ad56b769b6dc)

Describe two reasons aluminium is used in the compass body.
parts.markscheme:
Lightweight for easier handling during use <marks>1 mark</marks>

Resistant to corrosion from hand oils or moisture <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] per valid linked property.*

parts.content: State one environmental advantage of using aluminium in this product.
parts.markscheme:
Aluminium is recyclable and reduces landfill waste <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for a valid environmental advantage.*

parts.content:
The mechanical pencil body is made of injection-moulded ABS with a rubber grip.

![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/3f5414d3-3bb3-4ebc-90e4-8c57e2fff40d)

Explain how material combinations in this product improve functionality.
parts.markscheme:
ABS provides structure and toughness for the body <marks>1 mark</marks>

Rubber grip increases comfort and prevents slipping <marks>1 mark</marks>

Combination enhances both durability and ergonomics <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award up to [3] for valid links between material use and user benefit.*

parts.content: Discuss one limitation of using thermoplastics like ABS in products exposed to frequent mechanical stress.
parts.markscheme:
Can deform over time due to material fatigue <marks>1 mark</marks>

May crack under prolonged pressure or bending <marks>1 mark</marks>

Less structurally stable than metal under repeated use <marks>1 mark</marks>

Requires reinforcement or thick sections to improve performance <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for each relevant point up to [4 max]*

questions.specification:
What does this image demonstrate about smart materials?

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXe9tvdpjyf2dlwMP4s1NpqLThKMRQ2UW7UUhjUmhgn5gbVgpM8AJWvsiUES0YpKQTA3IsANEj48ZhwD9PaRfFkaPPUP2qgXqaxXoYYA1mr18jAXQFVK-W_bWIeoZXSAxMjVUJ2Z0A?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)
Which of the following materials is an example of a composite?
Which of the following is most likely to describe a physical property of glass?
What is a defining feature of mechanical properties in materials?

options.content: Response to applied force or stress
Ability to decompose under UV light

questions.specification:
Which best describes the classification of this example?

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXdeDK8agrJgw5YqWJ5b5xgKq4bCE8Rrenq1iR2fSZTPISrvlzrunmtw7heXHX2koMCCXoXN-tFF0p7avT1U1WzlQZ7r8LhvppdT8ZCkS-lDq_JC5LfxhIgSz2HlDrzCXNe9iZRi?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)
Which of the following is true of biodegradable materials?

options.content: They contain multiple material types fused together
They resist breakdown under natural conditions

questions.specification:
What challenge in material selection is suggested by this diagram?

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXcCcf4nK7FHyT-vBvmKQEWGEfzZken3pR1gJwjQ3pHQVhyCy7p_JmRvY1wc0EL8X3_ga_fmh8FxL1wuPuA3UwBAisTbMhyjQdohshp63YGHslDdhrp5zlOaZx2XEK9u8Bjfp_vyog?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)

Which material type is shown here?

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXeTiULyUYHI10EJZJsc3fGqEqWLXYD_vNivF0syldbSyGfkiU7RcWj2LM2gdgyB5H5Do_Hh0sab2t6edo-Jbd4LKt76mpbXIhh-oGALKsWT25KOUdM1rwzpYzVYJKthK1If9B-v?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)
Why might a designer choose a smart material over a traditional one?

options.content: To enable adaptive functionality in a changing environment

questions.specification: A sports product startup is developing a new commuter bicycle made primarily from sustainable materials. The goal is to reduce environmental impact while maintaining strength, comfort, and durability for daily urban use. The team is evaluating several options for the frame, tires, seat, and handlebar grips, using data from lab tests and supplier catalogs.

parts.content:
The designers are comparing natural, synthetic, and composite materials for various components of the bicycle.


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/91efe8ff-449c-4017-a836-65bcc87bf3a2)

Define the term “synthetic material” and give one example relevant to this bicycle design.
parts.markscheme:
- A synthetic material is one that is man-made through chemical processes, usually derived from petrochemicals. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Example: Synthetic rubber used for the bicycle tires. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Identify two reasons why the designers might choose a composite material for the bicycle frame.
parts.markscheme:
- Composite materials can offer high strength-to-weight ratio. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- They can be engineered to resist corrosion and fatigue. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
The team examines mechanical and physical property data of materials under consideration for the frame, seat, and grips.

![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/5030bcce-17e4-4e87-ab4b-d7c470fb5f4c)

Explain why the bicycle frame material needs to have high tensile strength and low density.
parts.markscheme:
- High tensile strength allows the frame to resist breaking under stress while riding. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Low density keeps the bike lightweight and easier to carry or accelerate. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Together, these properties improve both safety and user convenience. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Compare the physical properties of rubber and aluminium which are being considered for the bicycle handlebar grips.
parts.markscheme:
- Rubber: high elasticity absorbs shocks and provides a comfortable grip. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Rubber: low thermal conductivity means grips stay warm in cold weather. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Aluminium: low elasticity transmits vibration to the rider. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Aluminium: high thermal conductivity means grips can feel cold to touch. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
The designers are also exploring new smart and biodegradable materials to increase the sustainability and adaptability of the bicycle.

![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/28d9c3ab-bae2-43f9-baa2-597f78ee326f)

State one example of a biodegradable material that could be used in the bicycle seat padding.
parts.markscheme: - Example: Starch-based bioplastic foam. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Discuss the benefits and drawbacks of using smart materials in the bicycle design.
parts.markscheme:
- Benefit: Shape memory alloys could be used for foldable frame hinges, enabling compact storage. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Benefit: Photochromic paint could improve safety by changing color in UV light. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Benefit: Smart materials reduce reliance on electronics, simplifying product design. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Drawback: Smart materials are often more expensive to source. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Drawback: Some smart materials are not biodegradable or recyclable. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Drawback: Durability of smart materials may not match conventional alternatives. <marks>1 mark</marks>

questions.specification: Topic: Resource management and sustainable production; Sub-topic: Material properties.

parts.content: Evaluate the importance of chemical properties in selecting materials for food packaging.
parts.markscheme:
- **Reactivity:** Materials must be chemically inert or have low reactivity with the specific food contents, especially those with high acidity (e.g., tomatoes) or salt content. <marks>A1</marks>
- **Evaluation:** This is vital for maintaining food quality; high reactivity would lead to chemical contamination or degradation of the packaging (e.g., "pitting" in aluminum), making protective liners or alternative materials like glass necessary to ensure shelf-life and taste. <marks>A1</marks>

- **Toxicity and Leaching:** Chemical properties determine the degree of leaching or migration of substances from the package into the food (e.g., monomers, plasticizers, or BPA). <marks>A1</marks>
- **Evaluation:** As consumer health is the highest priority, selecting non-toxic materials is a critical constraint; this property dictates the strict use of "food-grade" materials to ensure compliance with legal safety standards and avoid toxic contamination. <marks>A1</marks>

- **Corrosion Resistance:** The ability of a material (primarily metals like tin-plated steel) to resist oxidation and chemical breakdown when exposed to the food product or environmental humidity. <marks>A1</marks>
- **Evaluation:** Corrosion resistance is essential for long-term storage; it prevents the loss of the hermetic seal and stops metallic rust from entering the product, which is critical for preventing food spoilage and maintaining safety throughout the product's life cycle. <marks>A1</marks>

*Award [1] for identification/description of a chemical property and [1] for a developed evaluation of its importance in the context of food packaging, up to [6 max].*
